data.body: TICKET-TRUNCATION failure mode (Joe): truncating the reviewed artifact invalidates the review of the full plan as it exists in rebar. AUDIT of truncation points: (a) child excerpts in final_gate (the bug — now FULL, no cap); (b) the plan itself fed whole today (risk only if a single ticket exceeds context); (c) ISF session log can be huge (supporting context); (d) agentic tool-result reads capped 20K (evidence-reading, bounded by design, agent re-greps — acceptable); (e) finding-text display caps 240-400 (cosmetic output, not review-invalidating). ALTERNATIVES (recommended hierarchy): (1) NO cap default — feed full plan+children (large context windows handle normal tickets); (2) STRUCTURAL DECOMPOSITION for over-budget containers (review each child/ticket at its level + epic ACs, aggregate — the proportionate-scrutiny model); (3) SECTION-CHUNK a giant single ticket by AC/section with FULL coverage (never drop the tail); (4) INDETERMINATE + coverage-recorded if uncoverable — never silently certify a partial review; (5) deterministic pre-retrieval ONLY for huge SUPPORTING context (ISF log), selection recorded as coverage. INVARIANT: never silently truncate the reviewed artifact; read the compiled rebar-show description; coverage recorded on the attestation so partial reviews are visible. Applied: full child bodies in harness + the invariant/alternatives in orchestrator child ca03. Committed.
